assertions: Exact intent match appears before description-only matches
Each result shows module, symbol, kind, and numeric OID
Exact symbol resolves directly
The page explains enum meanings without requiring tree navigation
Module qualification is preserved
Ambiguous unqualified alternatives are not silently selected
Exact numeric OID resolves
Scalar instance guidance shows the .0 suffix separately
Longest object prefix is selected
Instance suffix is preserved as integer arcs
Valid unknown OID is distinct from invalid syntax
No vendor or device identity is invented
Table, row, and index relationships are visible together
A concrete indexed OID example is shown
Notification varbind objects are listed
The UI does not suggest polling a notification OID
Direct imports are separated from transitive imports
Missing and cyclic dependencies have machine-distinguishable states
Source and revision are explicit for both variants
Added, removed, and changed definitions are separated
Module-qualified collisions remain separate
No result is auto-selected without an explicit ranking reason
Official source, acquisition time, and parse status are visible
Rights tier and permitted output scopes are visible
Unknown, not found, and unavailable due to rights are distinct
Restricted text and raw files are not returned
Missing import names the affected module
Diagnostics contain no raw third-party MIB text or local path
Value parsing occurs inside the browser
The scalar object and .0 instance are displayed separately
Unsupported lines are counted without inventing rows
Raw values are not logged or transmitted
The task completes within the recorded benchmark boundary
Peak memory and elapsed time are measured rather than claimed
All primary actions are reachable without a pointer
Focus order and visible focus follow result hierarchy
Generated command uses numeric OID and explicit placeholders
No credential, hostname, or customer identifier is prefilled
Responses are pinned to immutable release IDs
Rollback moves only the active pointer and does not mutate release contents
